title: Radiologist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI now screens and pre-measures the large majority of normal-study volume end to end; complex, ambiguous and interventional cases still require a radiologist.
rationales.toolMaturity: FDA-cleared imaging AI for triage, measurement and normal-study flagging is mature and embedded directly into PACS workflows.
rationales.adoption: AI triage and flagging tools are now common across large and tech-forward hospital systems, spreading into community radiology groups.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic pipelines can pre-screen studies, flag urgent findings and draft normal-study reports, with the radiologist signing off and handling complex reads.

tasksAtRisk: Screening standard imaging for common findings
Measurements and quantification on routine scans
tasksGrowing: AI model validation and quality oversight
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1224 (Radiologists), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI flags findings and triages urgent cases, but radiologists who interpret complex ambiguous images, perform interventional procedures, and validate AI outputs remain essential to patient safety.
